arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta's Arsenal legacy "up there" with Arsene Wenger if Gunners win Champions League
arts.sum: Arsenal winning the Champions League would "elevate" Mikel Arteta to Arsene Wenger and George Graham levels of fame, Gunners legend Nigel Winterburn tells Sports Mole.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta provides triple Arsenal injury update before Real Madrid
arts.sum: Arsenal head coach Mikel Arteta has provided an update on the fitness situation in his squad. This of course comes ahead of the Gunners’ highly-anticipated upcoming clash with Real Madrid.…

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta urges Arsenal to be 'bold and brave' against Real Madrid - as he insists the Gunners will play to WIN in Spain despite 3-0 aggregate lead
arts.sum: ISAAN KHAN IN MADRID: Mikel Arteta has roared on his Arsenal side to be 'bold and brave' when they face-off against Real Madrid at the intimidating Bernabeu tonight.

arts.ttl: ‘Impossible’ for Arsenal to block out Real Madrid narrative, admits Arteta
arts.sum: Talk of ‘remontada’ is rife despite 3-0 first-leg deficit Arteta wants Arsenal to try to ‘dominate’ in Europe Real Madrid’s history has loomed large with its epic comebacks but now it is time for Arsenal to write their own, Mikel Arteta says. For all the noise and the narrative, for all that many have crumbled here against the European champions, the Arsenal manager insisted that there is no fear and that his team are ready to step into the Bernabéu. He reminded everyone of a simple fact lost at times over the last seven days: they do lead this tie, 3-0. “If I had to pick one of the scenarios that I want the most, it would be this one,” he said. Asked if there was any fear, the Arsenal manager replied: “No, I wouldn’t use that word.” Instead, he talked of “respect, admiration and values, an inspiration for any team and any manager” and that was how he is framing this game. Not as something to be scared of, although he admitted that it would be “impossible” to completely block out the noise, and that the “narrative is fuel for them”, but something to aspire to: this is a night they must enjoy, he said, and one that he would like to play in himself. Continue reading...

arts.sum: Arsenal have no fear of facing Real Madrid at the Bernabeu and will play to win the game despite already holding a 3-0 advantage from the first leg, according to Mikel Arteta.
